rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed inside component implementation annexes for implementation-level signal routing bindings.
Use annex agree blocks to define signal routing assumptions and guarantees for implementation features.
generic_rules: Component implementation annexes are appropriate for binding signal flow and routing assumptions.
AGREE contracts should reference visible feature names and type declarations from the component scope.
Assumptions and guarantees should be scoped to the component type or implementation as appropriate.
Use visible identifiers to define signal routing relationships in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid, visible identifiers from the component scope.
Use boolean expressions to define range or conditional signal behavior in AGREE.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level signal routing contracts in component type annexes unless they are generic.
Avoid referencing undefined or non-visible identifiers in AGREE contracts.
